danger_signals.meaning: Bob is hunted as an illegal entity; threat from state enforcement

danger_signals.visual_form: Halo floats over possessed character's head
danger_signals.meaning: If spotted by enemies while possessing a body, player will be attacked; visibility = danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Satan's presence in mortal plane (inferred from narrative)
danger_signals.meaning: Supernatural threat level escalates; world state becomes more dangerous

danger_signals.visual_form: Faktur cyberpunk city and industrial zones
danger_signals.meaning: Dystopian environment communicates hostility and corruption of Earth

safety_signals.meaning: Ability to inhabit bodies and blend in; temporary concealment from threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Bob's status as angel sent by God
safety_signals.meaning: Celestial legitimacy frames player as force for order restoration

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Faktur cyberpunk city streets; pursuit behavior

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Dictator controlling Earth; experimental apparatus on dark side of moon
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Nuclear power station; moon facility with dimensional experiments

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Summoned from other dimensions; final antagonist
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dark side of Moon; dimensional breach; industrial destruction

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cyberpunk city Faktur in decay; Father Prime conducting illegal dimensional experiments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Police and military hunt Bob; citizens exist under authoritarian control
Threat level increases; Bob confronts final antagonist; reality destabilization implied

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Explosion destroys moon facility; Bob stranded on barren Moon
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Divine silence; God's abandonment of Earth communicated through lack of response

environmental_storytelling.example: Bob travels from Faktur cyberpunk city → industrial zones → nuclear power station → dark side of Moon facility, visualizing deepening corruption and supernatural infiltration
Cyberpunk city vs. industrial power infrastructure vs. experimental moon facility—each environment reflects the faction controlling it
Halo indicator over possessed bodies visually teaches player that inhabiting bodies is both tool and vulnerability; discovery = combat

environmental_storytelling.technique: Title and iconography as theological messaging
environmental_storytelling.example: Game's name 'Messiah' and Bob's angelic status with halo communicate redemptive arc, later subverted by Satan's deception

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Hunted by police and military; must find bodies to possess for progression and concealment
Can move through world as the possessed character; visible halo marks possession state; if spotted, body becomes combat target
Active pursuit and attack; indicates Bob's illegal status in world
Conducting dimensional experiments; represents authoritarian control and human hubris
Deceives Bob through false divine messages; escalates conflict; destroyed through facility sabotage

notes: Messiah uses possession as core mechanic tied to world-state communication—the halo signifies both player adaptation (blending in) and vulnerability (visibility = attack). The game's political messaging centers on three factions: Divine order (Bob), Totalitarian control (Father Prime/state), and Chaos (Satan). Environmental progression (city → industrial → moon) visualizes escalating supernatural threat. Religious imagery and controversy surrounding the 'Messiah' title itself becomes meta-commentary on the game's themes of divine authority vs. human corruption. The game's harsh tone (noted by reviewers as 'cruel' and 'sadistic') may be intentional manifestation of its dystopian world-state.
